:::note[Unverified — community/era build, adapted]
A classic Korean-community mage variant adapted to this shard. The **700/225 caps** are
source-verified against ServUO; the build shape, potion-throwing tactics, and PvP timings are
era wisdom and **field verification is pending**. Mark exact combo timings unverified and file
discrepancies per [wiki conventions](/guides/wiki-conventions/).
:::

In the Korean UO community a mage was called **마사** (*masa*, from 마법사 *mabeopsa*,
"magus/mage"), and there are a handful of classic 마사 variants. The **Alchemy Mage**
(**알케미 마사**) is the one that pairs spells with thrown **explosion potions** — the
"purples" — for a burst the enemy can't interrupt by hitting you.

This is one of three 마사 templates here, alongside the [Weapon/Halberd Mage](/templates/weapon-mage/)
and the [Stun Mage](/templates/stun-mage/). For the high-level PvP picture of all three, see
[PvP builds](/professions/pvp/); for the from-scratch caster storyline, see the
[Mage template](/templates/mage/).

## The idea: potion + spell alpha

A pure mage's burst is all spells — and a spell can be interrupted when you take a hit. The
alchemy mage carries **[explosion potions](/items/catalog/potions/)** and *throws* them.
A thrown potion isn't a spell: it can't fizzle and it lands while your next spell is casting.
The classic alpha is **throw purple → cast → the potion and the spell land almost together**,
stacking damage faster than the target can heal.

[Alchemy](/skills/alchemy/) is what makes the potions worth throwing: higher Alchemy raises the
**strength of the potions you brew**, so a GM alchemist's greater explosion potions hit for
meaningfully more than store-bought ones. You brew your own ammunition. See the
[Alchemist profession](/professions/alchemist/) for the crafting side.

## The 7 skills (≈700 total)

Seven Grandmaster (100.0) skills sum to **700.0** — this shard's total skill cap
(`Config/PlayerCaps.cfg`):

- **[Magery](/skills/magery/)** — your spells and your mobility.
- **[Evaluating Intelligence](/skills/evaluating-intelligence/)** — raises spell damage.
- **[Alchemy](/skills/alchemy/)** — stronger explosion potions (your second damage source).
- **[Resisting Spells](/skills/resisting-spells/)** — survive enemy magic.
- **[Wrestling](/skills/wrestling/)** — unarmed defense so a meleed mage still casts.
- **[Meditation](/skills/meditation/)** — refills mana between combos.
- **Flex: [Inscription](/skills/inscription/)** (spell-damage bonus + scribing) or another
  utility skill.

:::tip[120s come from power scrolls]
The seven-GM (100.0) ceiling is the *base* cap. Individual skills go to **120** via
**Power Scrolls**, which on this shard drop from **champion spawns and treasure** — see
[treasure hunting](/playing/treasure-hunting/).
:::

**Suggested stats (225 cap):** ~25 STR / ~10 DEX / ~100 INT (the 225 stat total is the shard
cap, `Config/PlayerCaps.cfg`). INT is your mana pool; this is a glass cannon, so range and
burst are your defense, not hit points.

## How it plays

- **Stock purples.** Brew [explosion potions](/items/catalog/potions/) at GM Alchemy and carry
  a stack; they are your interrupt-proof burst.
- **The combo:** open by throwing a potion, then chain Eval-boosted spells so they land on top
  of it. Exact PvP timings (potion arming/throw delay vs. spell cast time) are **unverified on
  this shard** — test and report.
- **Keep Wrestling up** so a melee attacker can't lock you out of casting, and **Meditate**
  between exchanges to keep mana for the next alpha.
- **Watch your own blast radius** — explosion potions hit an area; don't stand on the target.

## Money

Plays the [Mage template](/templates/mage/) money game — Recall in, burst things down, Recall
out — with potions accelerating kills. Your Alchemy doubles as income: greater heal, cure, and
explosion potions all sell, so a self-sufficient alchemist mage funds its own reagents and
bottles.
